aliases: Mumbai street burger with Schezwan sauce

english: Mumbai street burger with Schezwan sauce

region_of_origin: India (Maharashtra / Mumbai street food origin); served in Singapore
food_subgroup: Street food / snack — spiced potato fritter in bread roll with Schezwan chutney

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vada Pav or Schezwan Vada Pav. FOODON_03302050 (bread roll food product) is the closest defensible parent category covering the bread-roll component of this dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Vada Pav or Indian street-food fritter sandwiches. A0CKP (Bread rolls) is used as the closest parent for the bread component. The potato fritter filling and Schezwan chutney are not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept exists for Vada Pav or Schezwan Vada Pav as a food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 2 pieces of Schezwan Vada Pav, each piece consisting of one bread roll (pav) filled with a spiced deep-fried potato fritter (vada) and dressed with Schezwan chutney, totalling approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: Vada Pav — Nutritional composition estimated from ICMR-NIN component data: potato vada (deep-fried), bread roll (pav), and Schezwan chutney components; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for analogous fried potato patty and bread roll
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition); USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN or USDA entry exists for Schezwan Vada Pav as a composite dish. Values are estimated by combining ICMR-NIN data for deep-fried potato vada (batata vada), bread roll (pav), and Schezwan chutney in proportions consistent with a standard 140 g single-piece serving (280 g for 2 pieces). Schezwan chutney contribution to macros is minor but increases sodium. Energy and macros are consistent with published Indian street-food composition literature.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread roll and deep-fried potato products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white bread (GI 71) and deep-fried potato (GI 54–63); composite GI estimated at 65 weighted by carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Schezwan Vada Pav. Composite GI estimated at 65 using the weighted-average method: white bread roll component GI ~71 (University of Sydney GI Database, Foster-Powell et al. 2002, Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56) and deep-fried potato vada component GI ~57 (closest analogue: fried potato patty). Carbohydrate weighting: approximately 55% from bread roll, 45% from potato vada. Weighted GI = (0.55 × 71) + (0.45 × 57) = 39.05 + 25.65 = 64.7, rounded to 65. GL per serving = (65 × 96.6 g carbohydrate in 280 g serving) / 100 = 62.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1176 mg per 2-piece serving) from Schezwan chutney, dry garlic chutney, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Schezwan Vada Pav due to its high sodium load (~1176 mg per 2-piece serving), which represents approximately 51% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion reduction to one piece and requesting low-salt preparation is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (estimated GI 65, medium-high) and high carbohydrate load (~96.6 g per 2-piece serving) from refined white bread roll and starchy potato fritter
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with Schezwan Vada Pav as the combination of a refined white bread roll and deep-fried starchy potato fritter produces a high glycemic load (~62.8 per 2-piece serving), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; limiting to one piece and pairing with a high-fibre side may partially mitigate the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat from deep-frying in oil (~9.8 g fat per 100 g), with relatively low saturated fat (~1.4 g per 100 g) as predominantly vegetable oil is used; high sodium is an additional cardiovascular risk factor
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Schezwan Vada Pav presents a moderate fat concern due to deep-frying, though saturated fat is relatively low given the use of vegetable oil; the primary cardiovascular concern is the high sodium content (~1176 mg per 2-piece serving), and consumption should be occasional with attention to overall daily sodium and caloric intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Schezwan Vada Pav is a fusion Indian street food that combines the classic Mumbai street snack Vada Pav with the bold, fiery flavours of Schezwan (Sichuan-inspired) chutney. The dish consists of a deep-fried spiced potato fritter (batata vada) made from mashed potato seasoned with mustard seeds, turmeric, green chilli, and garlic, coated in a chickpea flour (besan) batter and deep-fried until golden and crisp. The vada is placed inside a soft white bread roll (pav) and dressed generously with Schezwan chutney — a pungent, spicy condiment made from dried red chillies, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and vinegar — along with dry garlic chutney and optionally tamarind chutney. The result is a sandwich-style snack that is simultaneously crispy, soft, spicy, tangy, and savoury. In Singapore, this dish is served at Indian fusion restaurants and food stalls, often as a snack or light meal. Nutritionally, a 2-piece serving (~280 g) provides approximately 680 kcal, 14.6 g protein, 27.4 g fat, and 96.6 g carbohydrate. The high carbohydrate and moderate-to-high glycemic index (estimated GI 65) make this a high glycemic load food, of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The sodium content is elevated (~1176 mg per 2-piece serving) due to the Schezwan chutney and seasoning, making it a high-concern food for hypertension. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly, but contains gluten from the bread roll.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Schezwan Vada Pav?
What is the glycemic index of Vada Pav?

Where can I get Schezwan Vada Pav in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No single approved source (ICMR-NIN or USDA) provides a direct entry for Schezwan Vada Pav as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining ICMR-NIN component data for deep-fried potato vada and bread roll with minor adjustments for Schezwan chutney. GI is an analogous estimate using the weighted-average method from University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN component data (batata vada, pav bread roll) and USDA FoodData Central analogues; no single approved source entry exists for Schezwan Vada Pav as a composite dish. 2. Serving size of 280 g (2 × 140 g pieces) is an estimate based on typical restaurant portion weights for this dish type; actual weights may vary by vendor. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero, as this dish uses no animal-derived ingredients (vegetable oil for frying, no eggs or dairy in standard preparation); confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN data for analogous fried potato preparations. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero, as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal products. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category and is not expected to be significant in plant-based fried street food. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. 7. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 65, medium category) using weighted-average of white bread roll (GI 71) and deep-fried potato vada (GI ~57) from University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Schezwan Vada Pav exists in approved sources. 8. GL per 2-piece serving = (65 × 96.6) / 100 = 62.8. 9. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record appears to be a field survey platform tagging error; Schezwan Vada Pav is a vegetarian potato-based dish with no chicken. This tag has been disregarded. 10. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this dish.
